llama-news.headline: Angela's Leaf Dancing Declared World Cultural Heritage
llama-news.summary: The Global Heritage Council voted unanimously to grant Cultural Heritage status to Angela's leaf dancing.
llama-news.body:
HERITAGE SUMMIT — The Global Heritage Council voted unanimously last Tuesday to designate Angela's leaf dancing as a protected cultural heritage practice, citing its profound influence on expressive art, architecture, and contemporary interface design.

Angela, whose secret power — Leaf Dancer — has evolved over fifteen years into a fully codified artistic tradition, performed a four-hour demonstration at the summit. Several council members reported they had never witnessed a performance of such precision and emotional range.

'A leaf does not fall by accident,' Angela said in her acceptance address. 'Every fall is a commitment. Every flutter is a question. I have simply learned to ask those questions with my body.'

The heritage designation means Angela's core techniques will be documented in the Global Archive of Expressive Arts. A dedicated school — the Angela Institute for Movement Sciences — will open in Patagonia next spring.
